---
title: "<span style='color: #0366D6;'>K-Nearest Neighbors</span>"
description: |
  A supervised learning algorithm<br/><hr/>
author:
  - Gloria Lowery
date: '`r Sys.Date()`'
format:
  html:
    link-external-icon: true
    code-block-bg: true
    code-block-border-left: "#31BAE9"
    code-overflow: wrap
    code-fold: show
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
course: IDC 6940 - Capstone Projects in Data Science
bibliography: references.bib
indent: true
---
------

[Presentation](link){.external target="_blank"}
 
# Introduction

The K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) algorithm is one of the simplest and most intuitive supervised machine learning methods used for both classification (assigning a category) and regression (predicting a value). It requires two instances for it to achieve its goal: a distance metric and defining K. 
 
## The Distance Metric
 
 
## Defining K 

## Strengths 

## Weaknesses 

## Uses 

In this paper we will apply the KNN algorithm to a data set of potential diabetic patients.  

# Method

# Analysis and Results

# Conclusion

# References
